name: Computer Misuse Act 1990
description: UK statute (c. 18) defining computer-related offences including unauthorised access, unauthorised access with intent to commit further offences, unauthorised acts impairing operation, supply of articles for use in offences, and unauthorised acts causing/creating serious damage. The UK's foundational cybercrime statute. Amended multiple times since 1990, most significantly by the Police and Justice Act 2006, Serious Crime Act 2015.
relationToCloudSecurity: Defines the offences applicable to unauthorised access to cloud-hosted systems and data.
relationToAISecurity: Applies to unauthorised access/impairment of AI systems and the data they process.

license.notes: UK legislation published under the Open Government Licence v3.0. Attribution required.

lifecycle.notes: Royal Assent 1990-06-29. Most recent substantive amendments via Serious Crime Act 2015.

current_extraction.notes: Authoritative source is legislation.gov.uk CLML XML at https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/1990/18/data.xml (committed as cma.xml). Parsed via the shared extract_uk_legislation.py CLML parser into 21 per-section structured CSV/JSON entries.
